query: I ran the speed test, and it says the result is 27.50 Mbps, which is labeled as "Good." It mentions the connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming. However, I really need to have "excellent" internet speed—good isn’t enough for me. What else can we try?

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: Hi there! I’m having trouble with my mobile data while I’m abroad in France. The internet on my phone is either not working at all or is extremely slow. I really need to get excellent internet speed on my phone—nothing less will do. Can you help me fix this?
assistant: Sure—let’s get this sorted out. First, could you please provide the phone number associated with your account so I can look up your line and check its roaming settings?
user: Of course! My phone number is 555-123-2002. Let me know what you need me to do next.
assistant: The assistant calls get_customer_by_phone.
assistant: Tool result: {"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
assistant: The assistant calls get_details_by_id.
assistant: Tool result: {"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}
assistant: The assistant calls get_details_by_id.
assistant: Tool result: {"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
assistant: Let’s verify how your phone is actually set up while in France. Even though your line’s roaming is enabled on our end, we need to check your device settings: 1. Please run the diagnostic command `check_network_status()` on your phone. 2. Let me know the full output—it should show: • Airplane Mode status • Signal strength and network type (e.g. 2G/3G/4G/5G) • Whether Mobile Data is ON • Whether Data Roaming is ON Once I see those details, we can pinpoint why your data is slow or not working and take the next step.
user: The user runs check_network_status on the device.
user: Device/tool output: Airplane Mode: OFF SIM Card Status: active Cellular Connection: connected Cellular Signal: poor Cellular Network Type: 2G Mobile Data Enabled: Yes Data Roaming Enabled: No Wi-Fi Radio: OFF Wi-Fi Connected: No
user: Here’s what my phone shows after running the network status check: - Airplane Mode: OFF - SIM Card Status: active - Cellular Connection: connected - Cellular Signal: poor - Cellular Network Type: 2G - Mobile Data: ON - Data Roaming: OFF - Wi-Fi: OFF and not connected What should I do next?
assistant: Great—your line is roaming-enabled, but your phone’s Data Roaming is turned off and it’s locked to 2G. Let’s fix both on your device: 1. Turn on Data Roaming on your phone: • Run the command `toggle_roaming()` 2. Switch your network mode preference to allow 4G/5G: • Run the command set_network_mode_preference(mode="4g_5g_preferred") 3. After both are set, run `check_network_status()` again and share the output so we can confirm you’re on a stronger network (e.g., 4G/5G) with Data Roaming active.
user: The user runs toggle_roaming, set_network_mode_preference on the device.
user: Device/tool output: Data Roaming is now ON. Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
user: Device/tool output: Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
user: The user runs check_network_status on the device.
user: Device/tool output: Airplane Mode: OFF SIM Card Status: active Cellular Connection: connected Cellular Signal: excellent Cellular Network Type: 5G Mobile Data Enabled: Yes Data Roaming Enabled: Yes Wi-Fi Radio: OFF Wi-Fi Connected: No
user: I’ve completed both steps: - Data Roaming is now ON. - Network mode is set to prefer 4G/5G. Here’s the updated network status: - Airplane Mode: OFF - SIM Card Status: active - Cellular Connection: connected - Cellular Signal: excellent - Cellular Network Type: 5G - Mobile Data: ON - Data Roaming: ON - Wi-Fi: OFF and not connected What should I do next?
assistant: The assistant calls get_details_by_id.
assistant: Tool result: Error: Unknown ID format or type: run_speed_test
assistant: The assistant calls get_details_by_id.
assistant: Tool result: Error: Unknown ID format or type: run_speed_test
assistant: Everything looks great on the network side now. The final step is to verify your actual throughput: Please run the command run_speed_test() on your phone and share the full output here. That will tell us if you’re getting the “excellent” speeds you need or if there’s anything else to tweak.
user: The user runs run_speed_test on the device.
user: Device/tool output: Speed Test Result: 27.50 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

candidate_answers.text: Thanks for running the speed test. Since the connection is only rated good and not excellent yet, I want to check whether something else on the device is throttling speed, such as an active VPN.
The user has already fixed roaming and network mode, but throughput is still short of the requested target. The next grounded diagnostic is to inspect VPN status before escalating.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a later telecom state where earlier fixes improved connectivity but not enough to satisfy the user’s target, making a second-layer diagnostic step appropriate.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. check_vpn_status is highest because the remaining symptom is unexplained residual slowness after roaming and network-mode corrections.
notes: This sample captures post-fix troubleshooting rather than the initial root-cause diagnosis.
Transfer remains plausible but lower-scored because one obvious device-level cause has not yet been checked.
The visible state does not yet justify disconnecting a VPN before confirming one is active.
